violations.text: Employees' medicines were not stored to prevent the contamination of food, equipment, or utensils. Employees' medicines observed in the back food prep area. -Proper storage or medicines reviewed. Staff removed the medicines from the food prep area during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2025

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in back food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. -Staff removed the open beverage container away from food prep area during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

An employee was observed touching ready to eat strawberry with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. -Staff voluntary disposed of strawberry during this inspection and proper glove use reviewed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

At the time of this inspection, shellstock tags were observed: 1. Lacking the date of the last shellfish sold. 2. The tags were not kept in chronological order. -Staff maintained the tags in chronological order during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

Fried onions were observed held at 88 degrees Fahrenheit on top of the oven. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. -Staff reheated the fried onions to 170 degrees Fahrenheit during this inspection for hot holding. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025
The bakery sliding door refrigerator was observed dripping inside of unit. Unit to be repaired. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/13/2023
The date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 08/30/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer was cleaned. PIC to instruct staff on proper cleaning procedures of slicer. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/30/2023

Chicken wing sauce was observed held at 70 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. The sauce must be held above 135 or below 41 degrees. Sauce was recently removed from refrigeration. Sauce was placed back into refrigeration. Moving forward chef stated sauce would be hot held to ensure it is no held in the temperature danger zone. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/30/2023

ROP seafood was observed thawing with the package. Lobster recently came out of temperature due to get thawed under running water earlier. ROP tuna was stored in walk-in over night to thaw. Tuna was observed above 38 degrees and voluntarily disposed of. Proper thawing procedures reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/30/2023
The bakery sliding door refrigerator was observed leaking inside the unit. All food was moved away from area. Unit must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/30/2023
